currentUse: Closed church retained by the merged Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish (Waterbury); no regular Masses, but listed as "open for weddings & funerals" through the parish office. The ancillary parish campus on John Street (school, former convent, garage, club building) was put up for sale via a City of Waterbury RFP in October 2025.
summary: The 1904 brick church at 46 Congress Ave (corner of John Street) is still standing and has not been sold or demolished. After the parish's suppression it was absorbed into Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish, whose current website lists St. Joseph as "open for weddings & funerals" by arrangement with the parish office — so the building remains in Catholic hands with occasional sacramental use, but no regular Mass schedule. In October 2025 the City of Waterbury issued RFP 8610-Wtby (posted Oct 7, responses due Oct 30, 2025) offering for sale the former St. Joseph Parish Corp. properties at 23-47 John Street — the school building, former convent, garage, and "club" building — while explicitly excluding the church building and its lot from the sale; no buyer or price has been published. In August 2023 parishioner Christian Allyn, working with a canon lawyer and members of Knights of Lithuania Council 7, filed a Vatican appeal against the closure; no published outcome of that appeal was found. The Knights of Lithuania Council 7, which formerly met in St. Joseph's school, now meets in the basement of Our Lady of Mt. Carmel Church at 785 Highland Ave. The story reached a national audience in April 2025 via "Little Lithuania," an episode of the podcast The Final Service about Allyn's fight for the church.

developments.headline: Parishioners file Vatican appeal to stop the closure
developments.detail: With closure set for the end of September 2023, parishioner Christian Allyn, working with a canon lawyer, appealed to the Vatican to halt the suppression of St. Joseph's. Lifetime parishioner Mary Gluodenis voiced concern about the archdiocese's stewardship of the property; the Archdiocese of Hartford said it planned to sell off the property. Weekly attendance was reported at 30-40.

developments.headline: National podcast episode on the fight for St. Joseph's
developments.detail: The podcast The Final Service released a 32-minute episode, "Little Lithuania," on Christian Allyn's appeal to save St. Joseph's Church in Waterbury. The episode description poses the appeal's outcome as an open question and does not report a ruling.

developments.headline: City of Waterbury offers former parish campus for sale — church excluded
developments.detail: The City of Waterbury posted RFP 8610-Wtby (responses due Oct 30, 2025, 10:30 AM) for the purchase and sale of the former St. Joseph Parish Corp. properties: a school building, former convent, garage, and "club" building at 23-47 John Street plus a portion of 46 Congress Avenue. The St. Joseph's Church building and its lot were explicitly excluded from the sale. No award, buyer, or sale price has been published.

sources.title: Purchase and Sale of Former St. Joseph Parish Corp. Properties (RFP 8610-Wtby)

developments.headline: Church retained by merged parish, open for weddings and funerals
developments.detail: The current Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish website (active as of its July 2026 88th Feast promotion) lists St. Joseph as "Open for weddings & funerals. Contact parish office" — confirming the building is standing, still held by the merged parish, and in occasional sacramental use, with no regular Mass schedule. Knights of Lithuania Council 7, which historically met in St. Joseph's school, now holds its regular meetings in the basement of Our Lady of Mt. Carmel Church, 785 Highland Ave, Waterbury.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (closed Sept 30, 2023 under the Hartford 2017 plan; grassroots revival did not protect it) is consistent with everything found: the WFSB August 2023 report describes the imminent end-of-September closure and the grassroots fight (Christian Allyn's Vatican appeal, Knights of Lithuania Council 7 involvement). Refinements: (1) the parish was merged into Our Lady of Mount Carmel Parish rather than simply dissolved, and the church building itself remains in that parish's hands, open for weddings and funerals — i.e., closed for regular worship but not yet alienated; (2) the disposition of the property is proceeding piecemeal — the John Street campus buildings (school, convent, garage, club) went to market via a City of Waterbury RFP in October 2025, with the church explicitly excluded.
gaps: The outcome of the 2023 Vatican appeal could not be verified — searched Dicastery/Apostolic Signatura rulings, 2024-2026 news, and Christian Allyn's name; no published decision found. The mechanism by which the City of Waterbury came to be selling the "former St. Joseph Parish Corp." buildings (purchase from the archdiocese/parish corp., price, and date of transfer) was not found in accessible sources; the RFP listing confirms only that the City offered them for sale in October 2025. No award/buyer/price for that RFP has been published as of this research. Waterbury Republican-American (rep-am.com) coverage is paywalled and could not be checked; Hartford Business Journal and marian.org returned 403s, and fox61.com timed out (fox61 headlines corroborate the Sept 30, 2023 closure but the pages were not successfully opened). A search-result snippet indicates Knights of Lithuania Council 7 will co-sponsor the order's 112th National Convention (July 31-Aug 2, 2026, Southbury CT), but the national organization's site was unreachable (DNS), so this is unverified. No evidence of demolition, formal relegation to profane use, or listing of the church building itself for sale was found.
